rounds.description: 整块从句 / 介词短语 / 修饰结构

tokens.v: Claire wants more news coverage and Paul wants free mall concerts. Both would make classical music more visible, but

tokens.answer: neither really solves the main problem:

tokens.v: young people find classical music hard to enter

tokens.answer: because it shows up without a story.

tokens.v: The most effective move is

tokens.answer: to put classical pieces inside the things they already watch —

tokens.v:
film soundtracks, video-game music, and short videos.
The reason is simple. People care about a song

tokens.answer: when the song is tied to a moment they already care about.

tokens.v: When Hans Zimmer used a Bach organ piece in Interstellar,

tokens.answer: listeners were not told to "appreciate classical music" —

tokens.v: they were moved first, and curious second, and streaming data shows

tokens.answer: they looked up the original within days.

tokens.v: One short Rachmaninoff clip

tokens.answer: used in a popular TikTok video

tokens.v:
can get tens of millions of plays in a single day.
Compare this with a mall concert. It is random, has no story around it, and is easily heard as background noise. News coverage has the same gap:

tokens.answer: it reaches people who already read news, not the young audience the question is about.
